chunks.title: Priori Analysis and Posteriori Testing
chunks.text: Let us talk about priori analysis and posteriori testing.

chunks.title: Priori Analysis and Posteriori Testing
chunks.text: These are the two terms that are used for algorithms and the programs.

chunks.title: Priori Analysis and Posteriori Testing
chunks.text: Priori analysis is done over the algorithm.

chunks.title: Priori Analysis and Posteriori Testing
chunks.text: So priori analysis means we will do the analysis of an algorithm by studying it into greater

chunks.title: Priori Analysis and Posteriori Testing
chunks.text: detail knowing how it is working and we get some results.

chunks.title: Priori Analysis and Posteriori Testing
chunks.text: The result of analysis there.

chunks.title: Priori Analysis and Posteriori Testing
chunks.text: What is that?

chunks.title: Priori Analysis and Posteriori Testing
chunks.text: We may find out the time and the space consumed by an algorithm.

chunks.title: Priori Analysis and Posteriori Testing
chunks.text: The result of analysis and testing is done on the program.

chunks.title: Priori Analysis and Posteriori Testing
chunks.text: So we run and execute the program and check it.

chunks.title: Priori Analysis and Posteriori Testing
chunks.text: So we will know the watch time it is taking, how much, how many seconds, milliseconds it

chunks.title: Priori Analysis and Posteriori Testing
chunks.text: is taking and also the amount of memory it is consuming in terms of bytes.

chunks.title: Priori Analysis and Posteriori Testing
chunks.text: So this is priori analysis is done on algorithm and the posteriori testing is done on programs.

chunks.title: Priori Analysis and Posteriori Testing
chunks.text: Now when priori analysis is done over an algorithm, we know well that algorithms are not written

chunks.title: Priori Analysis and Posteriori Testing
chunks.text: in a particular language.

chunks.title: Priori Analysis and Posteriori Testing
chunks.text: So they are language independent and also they are hardware independent.

chunks.title: Priori Analysis and Posteriori Testing
chunks.text: They are not meant for a particular hardware.

chunks.title: Priori Analysis and Posteriori Testing
chunks.text: But whereas a program we write it program that is hardware specific as well as language

chunks.title: Priori Analysis and Posteriori Testing
chunks.text: specific and as well as operating system specific, environment specific also.

chunks.title: Priori Analysis and Posteriori Testing
chunks.text: So they are dependent on the hardware as well as the programming language used.

chunks.title: Priori Analysis and Posteriori Testing
chunks.text: And here the result of this one is how we get the time and the space.

chunks.title: Priori Analysis and Posteriori Testing
chunks.text: We do not get watch time 3 minutes, 10 minutes, 5 seconds or milliseconds.

chunks.title: Priori Analysis and Posteriori Testing
chunks.text: No, we will get the time function and we get the space function.

chunks.title: Priori Analysis and Posteriori Testing
chunks.text: So this is the result.

text: Let us talk about priori analysis and posteriori testing. These are the two terms that are used for algorithms and the programs. Priori analysis is done over the algorithm. So priori analysis means we will do the analysis of an algorithm by studying it into greater detail knowing how it is working and we get some results. The result of analysis there. What is that? We may find out the time and the space consumed by an algorithm. The result of analysis and testing is done on the program. So we run and execute the program and check it. So we will know the watch time it is taking, how much, how many seconds, milliseconds it is taking and also the amount of memory it is consuming in terms of bytes. So this is priori analysis is done on algorithm and the posteriori testing is done on programs. Now when priori analysis is done over an algorithm, we know well that algorithms are not written in a particular language. So they are language independent and also they are hardware independent. They are not meant for a particular hardware. But whereas a program we write it program that is hardware specific as well as language specific and as well as operating system specific, environment specific also. So they are dependent on the hardware as well as the programming language used. And here the result of this one is how we get the time and the space. We do not get watch time 3 minutes, 10 minutes, 5 seconds or milliseconds. No, we will get the time function and we get the space function. So this is the result.
